The HPV vaccines are given as a series of either 2 or 3 doses over a 6 month period and can be given at many pharmacies throughout B.C. Supply of the HPV vaccine is guaranteed to be available at this location.

Provides holistic counselling, prevention, education, treatment, and support services to individuals with communicable diseases. Registered nurse provides professional health care and health promotion. Includes vaccinations, tuberculosis testing, prenatal and postnatal care, pregnancy testing, referrals to other community resources, and diabetes prevention information that includes a foot care nurse once a month.

Provides nursing services to the community, with a main focus on perinatal and immunization services and communicable disease control. Other services include education and programs regarding healthier lifestyles, maternal and child health, prenatal, high risk infants, school safety, injury prevention, HIV/AIDS awareness, care of elders, chronic disease, general treatment clinics, and emergency/after hour services. The community health nurse works closely with children in the community through educational sessions, immunizations, as well as prenatal, and teen health programs.

Provides prescriptions, health advice, and vaccine recommendations tailored to an individual's medical history and travel itinerary. Nurses provide vaccinations after an assessment with a doctor is completed. The clinic provides both travel and non-travel services and is designated as a Yellow Fever Vaccination Centre by the Public Health Agency of Canada.

Provides seasonal and routine vaccinations for children, youth and adults at Vancouver Coastal Health immunization clinics and community health centres. Travel and additional vaccines are available through travel clinics and most pharmacies for purchase. Flu vaccinations are available on a seasonal basis.

Offers a variety of services to support the healthcare and wellness needs of women in the Sunshine Coast area. Services include immunizations, pregnancy testing and counselling, birth control, emergency contraception, HIV and STI testing, PAP tests, and sexual assault services.

Provides health promotion, disease prevention, intervention, and support services to women, children, youth and families living in the Island Health region. Services are provided in homes, schools, health units and community settings. Offers a wide range of services including wound care, prenatal and postpartum support, pregnancy classes, child developmental assessment, immunizations, well baby clinics, hearing and vision screening, communicable disease control, and specialized nursing support services.

Provides support for pregnant women and new moms, their babies, children and families. Created by women, for women, the program is offered by public health nurses who will work in partnership with patients. Nurses are available by phone or in-person at Island Health public health units across the region. Services include prenatal information, postpartum physical and emotional support, breastfeeding support, family and community health referrals, child health clinics and immunizations starting at two months of age, and access to Baby's Best Chance handbook.

Offers protection against vaccine-preventable diseases in order to attain a high level of immunity that will eliminate or prevent outbreaks of vaccine-preventable diseases. Immunization services are offered at child health clinics, health units and various community locations, schools, individual appointments and at mass community clinics for administering influenza vaccine.
